meta.title: G29 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW Z4 G29 engine — B46, B48, B58. Real UK costs, honest reliability data, and a clear 70% BMW threshold. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW Z4 G29 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned G29 engine typically costs £2,000–£7,000 supply only [BMW-QUOTE], depending on engine — the B46 four-cylinder sits at £2,000–£3,500, the B48 at £2,500–£4,500, and the B58 straight-six in the M40i at £4,000–£7,000. Fitting adds £650–£1,040. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW Z4 G29 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B48 2.0-litre four-cylinder petrol in the sDrive30i (258 bhp) is the most reliable G29 engine — it's proven across BMW's modular engine family, avoids the B46's lower-output strain and the B58's additional complexity, while offering strong performance. The B58 is also robust but more expensive to replace.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW Z4 G29?

acceptedAnswer.text: Common issues include: fuel injector failure on the B46 and B48 four-cylinder engines (rough running, misfires, check engine light); cooling system failures (water pump, thermostat) on all B-series engines; and oil leaks from the oil filter housing and valve cover gasket on the B58 in the M40i.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW Z4 G29 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Overall, yes — the G29's B-series petrol engines are modern and highly reliable. The B48 has well-documented injector issues but they're manageable with proactive maintenance. The B58 is considered BMW's most robust modern straight-six. The G29 is built on a shared platform with the Toyota GR Supra, benefiting from Toyota's quality standards.

mainEntity.name: Which G29 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: We don't recommend outright avoidance — but a B46 (sDrive20i) without documented injector history is one to approach with caution. Injector failure is common and can lead to piston damage if ignored. For the M40i, ensure the oil filter housing and valve cover gaskets are leak-free.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my G29 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Key warning signs: B46/B48 — rough running, misfires, check engine light (injector failure), coolant loss with no external leak, overheating; B58 — coolant loss, oil leaks from the oil filter housing area, overheating. Use our diagnostic guide to identify the specific symptoms.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW Z4 G29 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply our 70% BMW threshold. A £4,500 B48 recon against a £25,000 Z4 is just 18% — well within the threshold. Even the M40i's £7,000 recon against a £35,000 car is just 20%. Unlike older BMWs, the G29's high retained value means engine replacement is almost always financially viable. Value the car first — but on a modern Z4, a recon is usually a repair decision, not a financial one.

mainEntity.name: How long do BMW Z4 G29 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B48 petrol can exceed 150,000 miles with proper servicing. The B58 straight-six is similarly durable — many examples exceed 200,000 miles in other BMW applications. The B46 is also robust when injector issues are addressed proactively. The G29's engine bay is well-packaged, making maintenance accessible for a roadster.

mainEntity.name: Is the Z4 G29 related to the Toyota GR Supra?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the G29 Z4 and the Toyota GR Supra were developed jointly. They share a platform, BMW-sourced engines (B58 and B48), transmissions, and suspension components. However, each car has its own unique tuning, styling, and character. The Supra is a fastback coupé; the Z4 is a convertible roadster. Both are rear-wheel drive.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW Z4 G29 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation; block stamping varies by engine. For the B46/B48 four-cylinder, the code is on the block near the exhaust manifold. For the B58 straight-six, it's on the block face below the inlet manifold. Tell us your registration and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted BMW specialists will confirm it for free.

hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the third-generation Z4 roadster — from the 2.0-litre B48 four-cylinder petrols to the 3.0-litre B58 straight-six in the M40i. engineDatabase.h2: BMW Z4 G29 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the G29 — from the 2018 launch to the present, including the 2020 engine updates.

confidenceScore.title: Confidence Score
confidenceScore.text: All ratings are provisional — no dedicated Engine Page exists for any G29 engine family. The B48 is considered the reliable sweet spot of BMW's modular engine family. The B58 is BMW's most robust modern straight-six. The B46 is effectively a B48 with different tuning — the same reliability profile applies.

overview.intro: The G29 is the third generation of the BMW Z4, a two-door roadster produced by BMW since 2018. It was introduced at the 2018 Paris Motor Show as the successor to the E89 Z4, marking the return of the soft-top roof to the Z Series sports cars. Built on a joint sports car architecture shared with the Toyota GR Supra, the G29 is powered exclusively by BMW's modular B-series turbocharged petrol engines: the B46/B48 2.0-litre four-cylinder and the B58 3.0-litre straight-six. All models are rear-wheel drive, with an 8-speed automatic transmission as standard. Production is currently scheduled to end in March 2026.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: BMW CLAR / Toyota-BMW joint sports car architecture • Predecessor: E89 • Successor: none (Z4 to be discontinued) • Sibling body styles in this generation: none — G29 is a 2-door roadster only.
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The G29 Z4 remains a desirable choice in the UK sports car market, with strong demand for the M40i flagship and the sDrive30i as the sweet spot in the range【PATTERN DATA】.

items.quote: Thin-Data Fallback mode — exactly one paragraph: The B58 3.0-litre straight-six in the M40i is the crown jewel of the G29 range — 340 bhp, silky smooth, and one of BMW's most reliable modern engines, with a soundtrack that justifies the premium over the four-cylinders. The B48 2.0-litre four-cylinder in the sDrive30i is the sensible choice: 258 bhp, proven reliability across BMW's modular engine family, and without the B58's additional fuel and maintenance costs. The sDrive20i's B46 is the entry-level option — 197 bhp is adequate, but the engine works harder in a car this size, and the fuel economy advantage over the 30i is marginal. For most buyers, the sDrive30i strikes the best balance of performance and running costs.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Entry Choice — adequate performance but the engine works hard.
Best Petrol — proven, reliable, and the sweet spot.
Performance Choice — robust but expensive to replace.

economicsRule.title: The G29 rule
economicsRule.text: A £4,500 B48 recon against a £25,000 Z4 sits at 18% — well within our 70% BMW threshold. Even the M40i's £7,000 recon against a £35,000 car is just 20%. Unlike older BMWs, the G29's high retained value means engine replacement is almost always financially viable. Value the car first — but on a modern Z4, a recon is usually a repair decision, not a financial one.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G29 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: sDrive20i — cheaper to buy and run, but the engine works harder.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G29 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: M40i — higher fuel consumption, higher replacement cost, but the best driving experience.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G29 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: sDrive30i — the sweet spot of performance, reliability, and running costs.

problems.title: B48/B46 Fuel Injector Failure
problems.affectedModels: G29 sDrive20i, sDrive30i (B46 and B48 engines).
problems.rootCause: BMW's B-series direct fuel injectors can fail due to heat-soak issues — when the engine is switched off, fuel in the injectors can evaporate, causing them to overheat and become stuck or leak. Symptoms include rough running, misfires, and check engine lights.
problems.recommendation: At the first sign of rough running or a misfire on a B48/B46 G29, have the injectors inspected immediately — a £400–£1,000 injector replacement is far cheaper than the £2,500–£4,000 engine replacement that follows if a failed injector causes piston damage.

cta.label: Compare B48 engine prices for your Z4 →

problems.title: B48/B58 Cooling System Failure
problems.affectedModels: G29 sDrive20i, sDrive30i, M40i (all B-series engines).
problems.rootCause: BMW's B-series engines use plastic coolant pipes and thermostat housings that can become brittle with age and heat cycling. Water pump failure is also common. The M40i's higher-output B58 places additional strain on the cooling system.

tieredCosts.work: Water pump replacement; coolant system inspection

problems.recommendation: Have the cooling system inspected at every service — a £400–£900 water pump replacement is far cheaper than the £2,500–£4,500 engine replacement that follows an overheating failure.

cta.label: Compare cooling system repairs for your Z4 →

problems.title: B58 Oil Leak Issues
problems.rootCause: The B58 straight-six is generally robust, but oil leaks from the oil filter housing and valve cover gasket can occur. These leaks are typically minor but should be addressed promptly to prevent oil starvation or fire risk.
problems.recommendation: On any M40i approaching 40,000 miles, have the oil filter housing and valve cover gaskets inspected — a £500–£1,200 preventative repair is far cheaper than the £4,000–£7,000 replacement that follows a major oil loss failure.

cta.label: Compare B58 engine prices for your Z4 M40i →

replacementCosts.h2: G29 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition
replacementCosts.note: No Generation variance note required — no Engine Page baseline exists for these engines yet. Costs are sourced from UK specialist suppliers [BMW-QUOTE].

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are BMW Z4 G29 only. The G29 is produced exclusively as a 2-door roadster — no coupé, saloon or estate variants exist. The Toyota GR Supra shares the same platform but is a separate model with different engine tuning and is covered on its own page.
